summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification for 16 open pull requests. No shared canonical was selected because the job is an inventory shard; all items are classified independently using the hydrated preflight artifact. No security-sensitive items were detected by the artifact.

actions.reason: Focused active contributor PR with supplied proof and passing checks; keep independently for maintainer review rather than close or merge.
Active focused feature/diagnostics PR with evidence and passing checks; classify independently for maintainer review.
The PR is plausible and related to message-delivery backlog, but failed proof/checks and unresolved bot-review detail block any close, fixed-by-candidate, or merge-style recommendation.
Maintainer-owned broad boundary-adjacent work should not be closed or merged by this plan shard; failed checks keep it as related maintainer work.
Draft mixed-scope PR with author follow-up pending; keep related and non-mutating.
Maintainer draft with focused config fix; not closable and not mergeable from this inventory plan.
Focused active PR with passing proof/checks; keep independently for maintainer review.
Small focused fix with passing checks; keep independently for maintainer review.
Broad maintainer-owned active PR pending author follow-up; keep related without closure or merge recommendation.
Focused review-ready contributor PR with sufficient proof; keep independently.
Small active feature PR with passing checks; keep independently for maintainer/product review.
Focused active PR with proof but author-waiting label; keep independently.
Focused test-only PR with passing checks; keep independently for maintainer review.
Docs PR is clean and passing, but merge is blocked by job mode/permissions and low-signal closeout is disabled; keep independently.
Broad active maintainer-labeled documentation/comment pass with failed checks; keep related as non-mutating backlog work.
Plausible focused PR, but failed behavior proof and unresolved bot-review detail block close, fixed-by-candidate, or merge-style recommendation; keep related.
